what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment record that links the dragnet-development channel to a board item (item-1149e45e) and records an attachment id, type, target URI, and creation timestamp.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter with no body content
target uses a board:// URI scheme pointing to item-1149e45e
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone offset
It belongs to the dragnet-development channel
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Stores channel attachments as markdown files
References external board items with a URI-style identifier
concerns: No descriptive content or context for the attachment
The board:// target cannot be resolved from this file alone
No author or reason for the attachment is recorded
notes: A bare metadata stub that records a board-item attachment in the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What does board item item-1149e45e represent?
Who added this attachment and why?
What semantics does attachment_type board_items carry?
purpose_vs_actual: The file functions as a metadata record for a channel attachment, matching its expected form, but it lacks any explanatory payload beyond the reference and timestamp.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a persistent, timestamped association between a channel and a specific board item for later retrieval or audit.
novelty: It is unusually minimal, containing only frontmatter with no body text or embedded symbols.
contribution: It contributes a canonical pointer and a timestamped channel-to-board-item association that a fused system would preserve as a reference edge.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record (id att-a1eab5e6) in the dragnet-development channel that points to board item item-1149e45e and records its addition time.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to persist a channel-to-board-item link for retrieval or audit.
